# dynamodb-toolkit ‚Äî Full Reference (v3)

> Zero-runtime-dependency, ESM-only micro-library for AWS DynamoDB built on the AWS JS SDK v3 (`@aws-sdk/client-dynamodb` + `@aws-sdk/lib-dynamodb`). Provides the `Adapter` class, expression builders, batch + transaction chunking, mass operations, a framework-agnostic REST core, and a `node:http` request handler.

This file is the long-form companion to `llms.txt`. Read `llms.txt` first for the API surface; this file documents semantics, edge cases, and the per-module helper API.

## 1. Adapter

`Adapter` (`src/adapter/adapter.js`) is the composition root. One Adapter typically maps to one logical entity (often one table). It owns long-lived state (`client`, `table`, `keyFields`, `hooks`) and delegates real work to the `expressions/`, `batch/`, `mass/`, and `paths/` modules ‚Äî all of which are independently importable.

### 1.1 Constructor

```js
import {DynamoDBClient} from '@aws-sdk/client-dynamodb';
import {DynamoDBDocumentClient} from '@aws-sdk/lib-dynamodb';
import {Adapter} from 'dynamodb-toolkit';

const client = new DynamoDBClient({region: 'us-east-1'});
const docClient = DynamoDBDocumentClient.from(client, {
  marshallOptions: {removeUndefinedValues: true}
});

const adapter = new Adapter({
  client: docClient,                  // required: DynamoDBDocumentClient
  table: 'planets',                   // required
  keyFields: ['name'],                // required: partition key first, optional sort key second
  projectionFieldMap: {},             // optional: alias ‚Üí real field for projections
  searchable: {name: 1},              // optional: '-search-<name>' lowercase mirrors
  searchablePrefix: '-search-',       // optional default
  indirectIndices: {},                // optional: {IndexName: 1} for key-only GSIs
  hooks: {                            // optional: per-instance hook overrides
    prepare(item, isPatch) { ... },
    revive(rawItem, fields) { ... },
    validateItem(item, isPatch) { ... },
    checkConsistency(batch) { ... }
  }
});
```

The constructor throws synchronously if `client`, `table`, or a non-empty `keyFields` is missing.

### 1.2 The Raw bypass marker

`Raw<T>` (single class ‚Äî the v2 `Raw` / `DbRaw` pair was collapsed) wraps an item to signal "I already shaped this for the DB; skip `prepare` and `validateItem`."

```js
import {raw, Raw} from 'dynamodb-toolkit';

await adapter.put(raw({name: 'X', '-internal': 'kept'}), {force: true});
const item = await adapter.getByKey({name: 'X'}, undefined, {reviveItems: false});
// ‚Üí instanceof Raw; item.item is the verbatim raw record
```

Marshalling (Set / Buffer handling) still runs unconditionally ‚Äî `Raw` only affects the toolkit's hook layer, not the SDK middleware.

### 1.3 Hooks

| Hook | Default | When called |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `prepare(item, isPatch?)` | identity | Before every write to add technical fields, derived columns, search mirrors |
| `prepareKey(key, index?)` | identity | Before every key-only operation; default keeps just `keyFields` |
| `prepareListInput(example, index?)` | `() => ({})` | Provides extra DynamoDB params for list/scan/query (KeyCondition, IndexName, ‚Ä¶) |
| `updateInput(input, op)` | identity | Last-chance hook to mutate the SDK Command input before dispatch. `op.name` ‚àà `'post' \| 'put' \| 'patch' \| 'delete'`; `op.force` set for `put` |
| `revive(rawItem, fields?)` | `subsetObject(rawItem, fields)` | After every read; strips technical fields, applies field subsetting |
| `validateItem(item, isPatch?)` | `async () => {}` | Async validator; throw to abort the write |
| `checkConsistency(batch)` | `async () => null` | Returns extra `make*` descriptors to bundle in the same `transactWriteItems` |

Hooks live in `this.hooks`. User overrides via `options.hooks` merge over the defaults; subclass overrides on `this.hooks` work too. Subclassing the Adapter and overriding methods is also supported.

### 1.4 Batch builders

Each returns a discriminated descriptor `{action, params}` (or `{action, adapter, params}` for `makeGet`). Compose with `applyTransaction` / `applyBatch`.

- `await adapter.makeGet(key, fields?, params?)` ‚Üí `{action: 'get', adapter, params}`
- `await adapter.makeCheck(key, params?)` ‚Üí `{action: 'check', params}`
- `await adapter.makePost(item, {returnFailedItem?}?)` ‚Üí `{action: 'put', params}` (with `attribute_not_exists` on the partition key)
- `await adapter.makePut(item, {force?, conditions?, params?, returnFailedItem?}?)` ‚Üí `{action: 'put', params}`
- `await adapter.makePatch(key, patch, {delete?, separator?, arrayOps?, conditions?, params?, returnFailedItem?}?)` ‚Üí `{action: 'patch', params}`
- `await adapter.makeDelete(key, {conditions?, params?, returnFailedItem?}?)` ‚Üí `{action: 'delete', params}`

`returnFailedItem: true` sets `ReturnValuesOnConditionCheckFailure: 'ALL_OLD'` on the request. Single-op CRUD: the thrown `ConditionalCheckFailedException` carries the colliding item on its `Item` field. Inside a `TransactWriteItems`: the matching `CancellationReason.Item` is populated. Pair with `explainTransactionCancellation` (see ¬ß3) to surface the failing descriptor + its pre-existing item in one call.

### 1.5 CRUD

- `await adapter.getByKey(key, fields?, options?)` ‚Äî options: `{consistent?, reviveItems?, ignoreIndirection?, params?}`. Returns `TItem | undefined`.
- `await adapter.getByKeys(keys, fields?, options?)` ‚Äî returns `TItem[]`. Missing keys silently dropped. Uses `BatchGetItem` with `UnprocessedKeys` retry.
- `await adapter.getAll(options?, example?, index?)` ‚Äî example is fed to `prepareListInput`. Returns `{data, offset, limit, total?}`.
- `await adapter.getAllByParams(params, options?)` ‚Äî params is the prepared input (KeyCondition, IndexName, etc.); options drives offset/limit/fields/filter/needTotal/reviveItems.
- `await adapter.post(item, {returnFailedItem?}?)` ‚Äî fails if key exists (`ConditionalCheckFailedException`).
- `await adapter.put(item, {force?, conditions?, params?, returnFailedItem?}?)` ‚Äî `force=true` skips the existence check.
- `await adapter.patch(key, patch, {delete?, separator?, arrayOps?, conditions?, params?, returnFailedItem?}?)` ‚Äî applies `UpdateExpression`. Key fields are stripped from `patch` automatically.
- `await adapter.delete(key, {conditions?, params?, returnFailedItem?}?)` ‚Äî DynamoDB Delete is idempotent; missing items return success.

`returnFailedItem: true` ‚Üí sets `ReturnValuesOnConditionCheckFailure: 'ALL_OLD'` on the underlying request. Thrown `ConditionalCheckFailedException.Item` carries the colliding item; inside an auto-upgraded transaction the matching `CancellationReason.Item` is populated.
- `await adapter.clone(key, mapFn?, {force?, params?})` ‚Äî read ‚Üí mapFn ‚Üí post (or put if force). Returns the cloned item or `undefined` on miss.
- `await adapter.move(key, mapFn?, {force?, params?})` ‚Äî read ‚Üí mapFn ‚Üí write + delete original via `transactWriteItems`. Returns the moved item or `undefined`.

### 1.6 Mass operations

All return `{processed: number}`.

- `await adapter.putAll(items, {strategy?})` ‚Äî `'native'` (default) uses `BatchWriteItem`; `'sequential'` does individual Puts.
- `await adapter.deleteByKeys(keys, {strategy?})`
- `await adapter.deleteAllByParams(params)`
- `await adapter.cloneByKeys(keys, mapFn?)`
- `await adapter.cloneAllByParams(params, mapFn?)`
- `await adapter.moveByKeys(keys, mapFn?)`
- `await adapter.moveAllByParams(params, mapFn?)`

### 1.7 Transaction auto-upgrade

Every write CRUD method funnels through `dispatchWrite(client, batch, checks)` (`src/adapter/transaction-upgrade.js`):

1. Build the batch descriptor (`makePost` / `makePut` / `makePatch` / `makeDelete`).
2. Call `hooks.checkConsistency(descriptor)`.
3. If the result is `null`, dispatch as a single `PutCommand` / `UpdateCommand` / `DeleteCommand`.
4. If the result is an array (even an empty one), bundle the main op + checks into a single `TransactWriteCommand`.
5. Throw `TransactionLimitExceededError` if total > `TRANSACTION_LIMIT` (100).

### 1.8 Indirect indices

Mark sparse GSIs that project keys only:

```js
new Adapter({
  client: docClient,
  table: 'planets',
  keyFields: ['name'],
  indirectIndices: {'climate-index': 1}
});
```

`getByKey({climate: 'frozen'}, undefined, {params: {IndexName: 'climate-index'}})` does:

1. `GetItem` against the GSI to extract the base table key.
2. `GetItem` against the base table for the full record.

`getByKeys`/`getAll`/`getAllByParams` also second-hop via `readListByKeys` / `readOrderedListByKeys`. Pass `{ignoreIndirection: true}` to skip.

### 1.9 List options shape

`getAll` / `getAllByParams` accept:

```ts
interface ListOptions {
  offset?: number;
  limit?: number;
  descending?: boolean;
  consistent?: boolean;
  fields?: string | string[];
  filter?: string;            // searchable substring
  caseSensitive?: boolean;
  needTotal?: boolean;        // designer-time; default true
  reviveItems?: boolean;
  ignoreIndirection?: boolean;
}
```

`needTotal: false` skips the `Select: 'COUNT'` pagination. `paginateList` still accumulates matches when a `FilterExpression` is set (DynamoDB's `Limit` is pre-filter, so naive use returns short pages).

## 2. Expressions (`dynamodb-toolkit/expressions`)

Pure functions. Each accepts a `params` object and returns a new params with the relevant expression strings + attribute maps populated. All handle name de-duplication, alias namespacing (`#upk0`, `:upv0`, `#sr0`, `:flt0`, `#pj0`, `#fbe0`, `#cd0`, `:cdv0`), and reuse of pre-existing `ExpressionAttributeNames` / `ExpressionAttributeValues`.

### 2.1 `buildUpdate(patch, options?, params?)`

Builds `UpdateExpression`. `options.delete: string[]` ‚Üí `REMOVE` clause. `options.separator` (default `'.'`) splits dotted paths. Pure-digit segments become array indices. `options.arrayOps`:

| `op` | DDB expression | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `append` | `SET path = list_append(if_not_exists(path, :empty), :values)` | Adds to tail |
| `prepend` | `SET path = list_append(:values, if_not_exists(path, :empty))` | Adds to head |
| `setAtIndex` | `SET path[i] = :value` | Absolute-index write |
| `removeAtIndex` | `REMOVE path[i]` | Absolute-index delete; DDB leaves a hole |
| `add` | `ADD path :value` | Atomic numeric increment / Set add |

### 2.2 `addProjection(params, fields, projectionFieldMap?, skipSelect?, separator?)`

Builds `ProjectionExpression`. De-duplicates and applies `projectionFieldMap` aliases. Sets `Select: 'SPECIFIC_ATTRIBUTES'` unless `skipSelect`.

### 2.3 `buildFilter(searchable, query, options?, params?)`

Builds `FilterExpression` over `searchablePrefix + field` mirror columns. Default mode: substring + case-insensitive. Options: `{fields?, prefix?, caseSensitive?}`.

### 2.4 `buildFilterByExample(example, params?)`

Builds an equality `FilterExpression` from a partial object (`#fbe0 = :fbe0 AND ...`).

### 2.5 `buildCondition(clauses, params?)`

Declarative `ConditionExpression` builder. Clause shapes:

```ts
type ConditionClause =
  | {path: string; op: '=' | '<>' | '<' | '<=' | '>' | '>='; value: unknown}
  | {path: string; op: 'exists' | 'notExists'}
  | {path: string; op: 'beginsWith' | 'contains'; value: unknown}
  | {path: string; op: 'in'; values: unknown[]}
  | {op: 'and' | 'or'; clauses: ConditionClause[]}
  | {op: 'not'; clause: ConditionClause};
```

### 2.6 `cleanParams(params)` / `cloneParams(params)`

`cleanParams` drops unused entries from `ExpressionAttributeNames` / `ExpressionAttributeValues` (anything no expression actually references). `cloneParams` shallow-clones params with deep-cloned attribute maps.

## 3. Batch (`dynamodb-toolkit/batch`)

All accept `(client, ...descriptors)` where descriptors are `{action, params}` items or arrays of items.

- `applyBatch(client, ...descriptors)` ‚Üí `Promise<number>` ‚Äî chunked `BatchWriteItem` (limit 25 per call), `UnprocessedItems` retry with backoff.
- `applyTransaction(client, ...descriptors)` ‚Üí `Promise<number>` ‚Äî single `TransactWriteItems` call. Throws if total > `TRANSACTION_LIMIT` (100). Accepts `{options: {clientRequestToken?, returnConsumedCapacity?, returnItemCollectionMetrics?}}` sentinel at any position in the variadic list for transaction-level knobs; multiple `{options}` entries merge, later fields winning.
- `explainTransactionCancellation(err, ...descriptors)` ‚Üí `{failures, message} | null` ‚Äî maps `TransactionCanceledException.CancellationReasons` back to input descriptors; returns `null` for non-cancellation errors so callers can early-exit in `catch`. `failures[i]` has `{index, descriptor, code, message?, item?}`; `item` is populated when the matching descriptor set `returnFailedItem: true`. Walks args identically to `applyTransaction` (null skipped, arrays flattened, `{options}` sentinels ignored).
- `getBatch(client, ...descriptors)` ‚Üí `Promise<{table, item}[]>` ‚Äî chunked `BatchGetItem` (limit 100), `UnprocessedKeys` retry.
- `getTransaction(client, ...descriptors)` ‚Üí `Promise<{table, item, adapter?}[]>` ‚Äî single `TransactGetItems`.
- `backoff(from?, to?, finite?)` ‚Äî generator yielding exponential delays with full jitter (default base 50ms, cap 2s).
- `TRANSACTION_LIMIT` ‚Äî `100`.

## 4. Mass ops (`dynamodb-toolkit/mass`)

- `paginateList(client, params, options?, needTotal?, minLimit?, maxLimit?)` ‚Üí `{data, offset, limit, total?}`. Accumulates matches when `FilterExpression` is set. `minLimit`/`maxLimit` defaults `10` / `100`.
- `iterateList(client, params)` ‚Äî async generator yielding raw page objects.
- `iterateItems(client, params)` ‚Äî async generator yielding individual items.
- `readList(client, params, fn)` ‚Äî call `fn(page)` once and return `nextParams | null`.
- `readListGetItems(client, params)` ‚Üí `{nextParams, items}` ‚Äî single page.
- `readListByKeys(client, table, keys, params?)` ‚Üí `Item[]` ‚Äî `BatchGetItem` wrapper.
- `readOrderedListByKeys(client, table, keys, params?)` ‚Üí `(Item | undefined)[]` ‚Äî preserves caller key order.
- `writeList(client, table, items, mapFn?)` ‚Üí `Promise<number>` ‚Äî `BatchWriteItem` puts.
- `deleteList(client, params, keyFn?)` ‚Üí `Promise<number>` ‚Äî read-then-batch-delete.
- `deleteListByKeys(client, table, keys)` ‚Üí `Promise<number>`.
- `copyList(client, params, mapFn?)` ‚Üí `Promise<number>`.
- `moveList(client, params, mapFn?, keyFn?)` ‚Üí `Promise<number>` ‚Äî chunks into 12 puts + 12 deletes per batch (24 ‚â§ 25 batch-write limit).
- `getTotal(client, params)` ‚Üí `Promise<number>` ‚Äî `Select: 'COUNT'` pagination.

## 5. Paths (`dynamodb-toolkit/paths`)

Pure path utilities over plain JS objects ‚Äî no SDK involved.

- `getPath(obj, path, defaultValue?, separator?)`
- `setPath(obj, path, value, separator?)` (mutates `obj`, returns it)
- `deletePath(obj, path, separator?)`
- `applyPatch(obj, patch, options?)` ‚Äî applies a patch with `delete` / `separator` semantics in memory.
- `normalizeFields(fields, projectionFieldMap?, separator?)` ‚Äî accepts string, comma-separated string, array, or object; returns `string[] | null`.
- `subsetObject(obj, fields, separator?)` ‚Äî picks fields at paths, returns a new object.

Pure-digit segments are array indices.

## 6. REST core (`dynamodb-toolkit/rest-core`)

Framework-agnostic helpers. Each parser accepts the raw query value (string or array) and returns a normalized shape. Each builder accepts a result/policy and produces a wire-shaped object.

### 6.1 Parsers

- `parseFields(input, {maxItems?})` ‚Üí `string[] | null`. `maxItems` default `1000`; extra segments truncated (DoS guard).
- `parseSort(input)` ‚Üí `{field, direction: 'asc' | 'desc', chain: SortClause[]} | null`
- `parseFilter(input, {mode?, caseSensitive?, maxLength?})` ‚Üí `{query, mode?, caseSensitive?} | null`. `maxLength` default `1024`; longer queries truncated.
- `parsePatch(body, {metaPrefix?})` ‚Üí `{patch, options}` ‚Äî splits wire body into raw patch + `{delete, separator, arrayOps}`. Patch accumulator is a null-prototype object (defends against `__proto__` in the request body).
- `parseNames(input, {maxItems?})` ‚Üí `string[]`. `maxItems` default `1000`.
- `parsePaging(input, {defaultLimit?, maxLimit?, maxOffset?})` ‚Üí `{offset, limit}` (clamped). `maxOffset` default `100_000` ‚Äî caps `?offset=` to prevent DoS via `?offset=1e15` driving `paginateList` into astronomical skip-page SDK calls.
- `parseFlag(input)` ‚Üí `boolean` ‚Äî accepts `yes` / `true` / `1` / `on` (case-insensitive)

### 6.2 Builders

- `buildEnvelope(result, {keys?, links?})` ‚Üí wraps `{data, offset, limit, total?}` with configurable key names; optionally embeds `links`.
- `buildErrorBody(err, {includeDebug?, errorId?})` ‚Üí `{code, message, errorId?, stack?}`
- `paginationLinks(offset, limit, total, urlBuilder?)` ‚Üí `{prev: string | null, next: string | null}` ‚Äî `urlBuilder` is `({offset, limit}) => string`.

### 6.3 Policy

`defaultPolicy`:

```js
{
  metaPrefix: '_',
  dbPrefix: '-',
  methodPrefix: '-',
  envelope: {items: 'data', total: 'total', offset: 'offset', limit: 'limit', links: 'links'},
  statusCodes: {miss: 404, validation: 422, consistency: 409, throttle: 429, transient: 503, internal: 500},
  errorBody: buildErrorBody,
  needTotal: true,
  defaultLimit: 10,
  maxLimit: 100,
  maxOffset: 100_000
}
```

- `mapErrorStatus(err, statusCodes?)` ‚Üí number ‚Äî maps SDK error names + `$metadata.httpStatusCode` to HTTP status per policy.
- `mergePolicy(overrides)` ‚Üí deep-merged `RestPolicy`.

## 7. Handler (`dynamodb-toolkit/handler`)

```js
import {createServer} from 'node:http';
import {createHandler} from 'dynamodb-toolkit/handler';

const handler = createHandler(adapter, {
  policy: {/* override defaults */},
  sortableIndices: {name: '-t-name-index'},
  keyFromPath: (rawKey, adapter) => ({[adapter.keyFields[0]]: rawKey}),
  exampleFromContext: (query, body) => ({}),
  maxBodyBytes: 1024 * 1024 // default 1 MB; bodies exceeding ‚Üí 413 PayloadTooLarge
});

createServer(handler).listen(3000);
```

### 7.1 Standard route pack

| Method | URL | Adapter call |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `GET` | `/` | `getAll` + envelope |
| `POST` | `/` | `post` (204) |
| `DELETE` | `/?filter=‚Ä¶` | `deleteAllByParams` (`{processed}`) |
| `GET` | `/-by-names?names=‚Ä¶&fields=‚Ä¶` | `getByKeys` (plain array) |
| `DELETE` | `/-by-names?names=‚Ä¶` | `deleteByKeys` (`{processed}`) |
| `PUT` | `/-load` | `putAll` (body: array; `{processed}`) |
| `PUT` | `/-clone` / `/-move` | `cloneAllByParams` / `moveAllByParams` (body: overlay; `{processed}`) |
| `PUT` | `/-clone-by-names?names=‚Ä¶` / `/-move-by-names?names=‚Ä¶` | `cloneByKeys` / `moveByKeys` (body: overlay) |
| `GET` | `/:key` | `getByKey` (404 on miss) |
| `PUT` | `/:key?force=true` | `put` (204) |
| `PATCH` | `/:key` | `patch` (body: `{field, _delete, _separator, _arrayOps}`) |
| `DELETE` | `/:key` | `delete` (204, idempotent) |
| `PUT` | `/:key/-clone` / `/:key/-move` | single-item `clone` / `move` |

Method prefix `-` is configurable via `policy.methodPrefix`. `keyFromPath` lets you map composite keys; default uses `adapter.keyFields[0]`.

### 7.2 Query parameters

- `?fields=name,climate` ‚Äî projection
- `?filter=tooine` ‚Äî `searchable` substring filter
- `?sort=name` / `?sort=-name` ‚Äî ascending / descending; resolved via `sortableIndices`
- `?offset=N&limit=N` ‚Äî pagination
- `?consistent=yes|true|1|on` ‚Äî strong consistency
- `?force=yes|true|1|on` ‚Äî overwrite without existence check on `put`/`clone`/`move`

### 7.3 Error mapping

Per `policy.statusCodes`. Default: `ConditionalCheckFailedException` / `TransactionCanceledException` / `TransactionConflictException` ‚Üí 409, `ValidationException` ‚Üí 422, `ProvisionedThroughputExceededException` / `RequestLimitExceeded` ‚Üí 429, 5xx SDK or `InternalServerError` ‚Üí 503, anything else ‚Üí 500.

`policy.errorBody` builds the response body. Default returns `{code, message}`.

## 8. Bare files

- `import {sleep} from 'dynamodb-toolkit'` ‚Äî `sleep(ms): Promise<void>`
- `import {seq} from 'dynamodb-toolkit'` ‚Äî sequential async runner
- `import {random} from 'dynamodb-toolkit'` ‚Äî small random helpers

## 9. Testing recipes

```js
import {mock} from 'node:test';
import {Adapter, raw} from 'dynamodb-toolkit';

const send = mock.fn(async cmd => {
  if (cmd.constructor.name === 'GetCommand') return {Item: {name: 'X'}};
  return {};
});
const adapter = new Adapter({client: {send}, table: 'T', keyFields: ['name']});
```

For end-to-end against DynamoDB Local, see `tests/helpers/dynamodb-local.js` ‚Äî it spawns `amazon/dynamodb-local` via Docker on a random port and skips with a clear message when Docker is unavailable.

## 10. Migration from v2

- AWS SDK v3 peer-deps replace `aws-sdk` v2.
- One data format (plain JS via `lib-dynamodb`); `Raw` / `DbRaw` collapsed to `Raw<T>`.
- Options bags everywhere ‚Äî `put(item, {force: true})`, `patch(key, patch, {delete: [...]})`.
- Hooks renamed: `prepareListParams` ‚Üí `prepareListInput`, `updateParams` ‚Üí `updateInput`.
- Patch wire format: `_delete` / `_separator` (single underscore by default; configurable via `policy.metaPrefix`).
- REST split into `rest-core` (framework-agnostic) + `handler` (`node:http`). Koa lives in a separate package.
- Dropped: `makeClient`, `getProfileName`, `specialTypes`, `converter`, `converterOptions`. Use `@aws-sdk/credential-providers` (`fromIni`, `fromNodeProviderChain`) directly.
- `generic*` mass methods folded into `options.strategy: 'native' | 'sequential'`.
- Transaction limit raised 25 ‚Üí 100.
- v2 documentation snapshot lives in the wiki repo at the `v2.3-docs` git tag. v2 source remains available on npm as `dynamodb-toolkit@2.3.0`.
